Action: Approved a contract, via a piggybacking of the School District's bid, with Harvard Jolly Architecture in the amount of $98,000 to provide architectural and engineering services for additional athletic facilities at the proposed new School District of Lee County PreK-8 School on Three Oaks Parkway, approve a contingency fund amount of $9,800 (an amount equal to 10% of the total project cost) to cover unforeseen circumstanc...
ActionTaken: Approved a contract, via a piggybacking of the School District's bid, with Harvard Jolly Architecture in the amount of $98,000 to provide architectural and engineering services for additional athletic facilities at the proposed new School District of Lee County PreK-8 School on Three Oaks Parkway, approve a contingency fund amount of $9,800 (an amount equal to 10% of the total project cost) to cover unforeseen circumstances which may occur, and authorize the Village Manager to execute the contract and any ancillary paperwork which may be required on behalf of the Village of Estero Council
Outcome: Approved a contract, via a piggybacking of the School District's bid, with Harvard Jolly Architecture in the amount of $98,000 to provide architectural and engineering services for additional athletic facilities at the proposed new School District of Lee County PreK-8 School on Three Oaks Parkway, approve a contingency fund amount of $9,800 (an amount equal to 10% of the total project cost) to cover unforeseen circumstances which may occur, and authorize the Village Manager to execute the contract and any ancillary paperwork which may be required on behalf of the Village of Estero Council
MotionText: Approved a contract, via a piggybacking of the School District's bid, with Harvard Jolly Architecture in the amount of $98,000 to provide architectural and engineering services for additional athletic facilities at the proposed new School District of Lee County PreK-8 School on Three Oaks Parkway, approve a contingency fund amount of $9,800 (an amount equal to 10% of the total project cost) to cover unforeseen circumstances which may occur, and authorize the Village Manager to execute the contract and any ancillary paperwork which may be required on behalf of the Village of Estero Council

ProjectTitle: Discussion: Location ofTwo New School Facilities in Estero
Summary: Discussion: Location ofTwo New School Facilities in Estero. Action: No formal action recorded. Presentation/Comments: Chris Patricca, District 3 representative on the School District ofLee County School Board. The purpose ofthe discussion was to determine whether there was consensus among Councilmembers on where the School District should locate their two new facilities. Options co...
ActionTaken: No formal action recorded. Discussion: Location ofTwo New School Facilities in Estero Staff Presentation/Comments: Village Manager Sarkozy. Presentation/Comments: Chris Patricca, District 3 representative on the School District ofLee County School Board. The purpose ofthe discussion was to determine whether there was consensus among Councilmembers on where the School District should locate their two new facilities. Options considered were: (1) locating adjacent to the Estero High School site on Williams Road; and, (2) building on a Three Oaks Parkway 70-acre site. Council Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Boesch, Batos, Levitan, Wilson, McLain, Vice Mayor Errington and Mayor Ribble. Consensus ofthe Council indicated four in favor ofthe Three Oaks Parkway site and three in favor ofthe Williams Road location. Public Comment: Ed Weil, Rookery Pointe John Russell, Estero Palms The workshop session went into recess
Outcome: No formal action recorded. Discussion: Location ofTwo New School Facilities in Estero Staff Presentation/Comments: Village Manager Sarkozy.
